# App Store Review Fixes - Implementation Plan

**Review Date:** February 20, 2026
**Submission ID:** 758a340d-f3e9-4781-b231-fe25ceb90da5

## Issues to Fix

### 1. **Guideline 1.4.1 - Safety - Physical Harm** ⚠️
**Issue:** App provides health/medical recommendations without citations
**Current State:** Recommendations are generated by OpenAI but no sources are provided
**Action Required:** Add citations/links to sources for medical recommendations

### 2. **Guidelines 5.1.1(i) & 5.1.2(i) - Legal - Privacy** ⚠️
**Issue:** App shares personal data with third-party AI service (OpenAI) without:
- Clear explanation of what data is sent
- Identification of who data is sent to
- User permission before sharing
- Privacy policy documentation

**Current State:** 
- Personal data is sent to OpenAI without user consent dialog
- Privacy policy exists at https://gofitai.org/privacy-policy (needs update)
- No in-app disclosure of data sharing

**Action Required:**
1. Add clear in-app disclosure before sending data to AI
2. Get user permission before first recommendation
3. Identify all data being sent to OpenAI
4. Update privacy policy to document this

---

## Implementation Steps

### Phase 1: Add Citations to Recommendations

**Files to Modify:**
- `/backend/routes/recommendations.js` - Add source URLs to meals and exercises

**Changes:**
1. Modify `RecommendationMealItem` model to include `sources` array
2. Modify `Exercise` model to include `sources` array  
3. Update AI prompt to include request for citing sources
4. Update frontend to display sources/citations to users

**Frontend Changes:**
- `/GoFit.Ai - live Healthy/Features/Workout/WorkoutSuggestionsView.swift` - Display sources
- Show "Learn More" links in meal and exercise cards

---

### Phase 2: Add Privacy Disclosure & User Permission

**Files to Modify:**
- `/backend/routes/recommendations.js` - Add consent tracking
- `/GoFit.Ai - live Healthy/Features/Workout/WorkoutSuggestionsView.swift` - Add disclosure sheet
- User model - Track AI consent preferences

**Changes:**
1. Create privacy disclosure UI (Swift view)
2. Show disclosure before first AI recommendation
3. Get user permission to share data with OpenAI
4. Save consent status in database
5. Add endpoint to fetch current AI consent status

**What Data Gets Sent to OpenAI:**
- User profile: name, age, goals, activity level
- Dietary preferences: vegan, vegetarian, allergies, restrictions
- Physical metrics: weight, height, target weight, target calories/macros
- Onboarding data: favorite cuisines, foods, cooking skill, budget
- Machine Learning insights: user type, learned preferences
- Recent meal history: last 5 meals with nutritional data

---

### Phase 3: Update Privacy Policy

**File to Update:**
- Privacy policy at: https://gofitai.org/privacy-policy

**Content to Add:**
1. **AI Data Sharing Section:**
   - What data is shared: List all personal data fields
   - Who it's shared with: OpenAI (GPT-4o API)
   - Purpose: Generate personalized meal and workout recommendations
   - Data protection: OpenAI compliance with privacy standards
   
2. **User Control Section:**
   - How to manage AI recommendations
   - How to opt-out
   - Data retention and deletion

---

## Detailed Changes

### 1. Backend - Add Source Field to Recommendation Models

**File:** `/backend/models/Recommendation.js`
- Add optional `sources` array field to meal items
- Add optional `sources` array field to exercises

**File:** `/backend/routes/recommendations.js`
- Update AI prompt to request sources/citations
- Modify response parsing to extract and store sources

### 2. Frontend - Add Privacy Disclosure Sheet

**File:** `/GoFit.Ai - live Healthy/Features/Workout/WorkoutSuggestionsView.swift`
- Create new `PrivacyDisclosureView.swift`
- Show sheet on first recommendation load
- Add accept/decline buttons
- Save user's choice locally and to backend

### 3. Frontend - Display Citations

**File:** `/GoFit.Ai - live Healthy/Features/Workout/WorkoutSuggestionsView.swift`
- Show "Sources" disclosure group in meal/exercise cards
- Display clickable links to sources
- Format: "Learn more about [nutrition guideline]"

### 4. API Endpoints

**New Endpoint:** `GET /api/recommendations/ai-consent`
- Get user's current AI consent status
- Return: `{ aiConsent: boolean }`

**Modified Endpoint:** `GET /api/recommendations/daily`
- Check if user has given consent for AI
- If not consented: Show disclosure first
- If consented: Generate recommendations

---

## Data Fields Sent to OpenAI

The following personal data is sent to OpenAI GPT-4o when generating recommendations:

```
USER PROFILE DATA:
- name
- age (if available)
- goals
- activityLevel
- dietaryPreferences (array)
- allergies (array)
- weight (kg)
- height (cm)
- target weight
- target calories
- target protein/carbs/fat

ONBOARDING DATA:
- workoutPreferences
- favoriteCuisines
- foodPreferences
- favoriteFoods
- mealTimingPreference
- cookingSkill
- budgetPreference
- motivationLevel
- lifestyleFactors
- drinkingFrequency
- smokingStatus
- fastingPreference

ML INSIGHTS:
- userType
- learned favorite foods
- preferred meal times
- average meal calories
- preferred macro ratio

RECENT ACTIVITY:
- Last 5 meals
  - Items
  - Calories
  - Protein/carbs/fat
  - Timestamp
```

---

## Privacy Policy Update Template

### Section: "AI-Powered Recommendations & Data Sharing"

We use artificial intelligence to provide personalized meal and workout recommendations. To deliver this service, we share certain personal data with OpenAI's GPT-4o API.

**What Data is Shared:**
- Your profile information (name, goals, activity level, physical metrics)
- Your dietary preferences and allergies
- Your food preferences and favorite cuisines
- Your recent meal history (last 5 meals)
- Machine learning insights from your usage patterns

**Who it's Shared With:**
- OpenAI Inc. (GPT-4o API service)
- OpenAI's privacy policy: https://openai.com/privacy-policy

**Purpose:**
- Generate personalized daily meal plans
- Create customized workout routines
- Provide nutrition and fitness insights

**Your Control:**
- You can opt-out of AI recommendations at any time
- You can request deletion of data shared with AI
- Disabling recommendations prevents data sharing with OpenAI

**Data Security:**
- OpenAI uses enterprise-grade encryption
- Data is transmitted over HTTPS
- No data is permanently stored by OpenAI for recommendation generation

---

## Testing Checklist

- [ ] Meal items display sources/citations
- [ ] Exercise items display sources/citations
- [ ] Privacy disclosure appears on first recommendation
- [ ] Accept/decline buttons work correctly
- [ ] Consent is saved and remembered
- [ ] Privacy policy is updated and accessible
- [ ] All links in citations are clickable and valid
- [ ] Data sharing stops if user declines consent
- [ ] App Store submission passes review
